title: Earnings Call Transcript: Opendoor beats Q4 2024 EPS forecast, stock dips
text: Opendoor Technologies exceeded Q4 2024 expectations with an EPS of -$0.16 (forecast: -$0.17) and revenue of $1.08 billion (forecast: $965.32 million). Despite beating earnings, the stock declined 7.14% in after-hours trading. CEO Carrie Wheeler outlined four focus areas for improving the cash offer business: Spread Optimization with increased spreads in January to manage risk, Enhanced Customer Experience through improved pricing models with over 70% of 2024 acquisitions from sellers who declined their first offer but later accepted a refresh, Seasonal Marketing Strategy realigning marketing spend to Q1 and Q4 when spreads are lower, and Operational Discipline through cost efficiency programs and workforce reductions. Expanded offerings include Lists with Opendoor in nearly all markets and Marketplace launched in Charlotte and Raleigh. The company remains focused on delivering 5-7% annual contribution margin while maintaining financial discipline. For Q1 2025 guidance, revenue of $1.0-$1.075 billion with contribution profit of $40-50 million, homes over 3,500, adjusted operating expenses of ~$90 million, and stock-based compensation down 50% year-over-year to $13-15 million.
